title: The Berkeley Gazette, November 11, 1977, Page 5
author: The Berkeley Gazette
card_summary: Page 5 of The Berkeley Gazette (Berkeley, California), dated Friday, November 11, 1977. By 1977 Philip K. Dick was living in Santa Ana, California, and was in the midst of writing the VALIS trilogy material and his Exegesis.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from The Berkeley Gazette (Berkeley, California), dated Friday, November 11, 1977, page 5. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 15, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

By November 1977, Dick was living in Orange County and deep into the writing of his Exegesis, processing the 2-3-74 mystical experiences that had occurred three years earlier. Though Dick had long since left Berkeley, the city remained important to his identity and literary imagination. The Berkeley Gazette continued to serve as the local paper for a city that had become synonymous with counterculture and intellectual ferment. The specific content of this page remains to be determined through examination of the scanned image.
